terms.definition: Molecules in which a central atom is surrounded by four atoms arranged in a tetrahedral geometry.
A notation based on the Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion theory used to predict the shape of molecules.
Forces that occur between electron pairs due to their negative charges, causing them to move as far apart as possible.
A bond that lies in the same geometric plane as other atoms or bonds in a molecule.
A three-dimensional molecular shape with four bonds around a central atom and bond angles of about 109.5°.
Atoms located at the outer ends of a molecule and directly bonded to the central atom.
Pairs of valence electrons that are not involved in chemical bonding and remain on a single atom.
An electron found in the outermost energy level of an atom that participates in chemical bonding.
